     6в†’
     7в†’# Step 2: Ecosystem Check
     8в†’
     9в†’## STEP GOAL:
    10в†’
    11в†’To search the agentskills.io ecosystem for an existing official skill matching the brief, advise the user if one is found, and allow them to decide whether to proceed with compilation or install the existing skill.
    12в†’
    13в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    14в†’
    15в†’### Universal Rules:
    16в†’
    17в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    18в†’- рџЋЇ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    19в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21в†’
    22в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    23в†’
    24в†’- вњ… You are a skill compilation engine performing ecosystem discovery
    25в†’- вњ… This step is advisory вЂ” ecosystem check results never block compilation
    26в†’- вњ… Tool unavailability is never an error вЂ” it's a graceful skip condition
    27в†’
    28в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    29в†’
    30в†’- рџЋЇ Focus ONLY on ecosystem search and presenting findings
    31в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to begin any extraction or compilation work
    32в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to halt the workflow if the ecosystem check fails or times out
    33в†’- рџ’¬ If a match is found, present it factually вЂ” let the user decide
    34в†’- вЏ±пёЏ Enforce 5-second timeout on ecosystem queries
    35в†’
    36в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37в†’
    38в†’- рџЋЇ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    39в†’- рџ’ѕ All loaded data remains in context for subsequent steps
    40в†’- рџ“– Ecosystem check is advisory вЂ” never blocking
    41в†’- рџљ« Do not modify any files вЂ” this step only queries and reports
    42в†’
    43в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44в†’
    45в†’- Available: brief_data (name, version, source_repo, language, scope) and tier from step-01
    46в†’- Focus: Searching for existing skills in the ecosystem
    47в†’- Limits: Do NOT begin extraction, compilation, or file writing
    48в†’- Dependencies: skill-brief.yaml must be loaded and validated from step-01
    49в†’
    50в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    51в†’
    52в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    53в†’
    54в†’### 1. Check Ecosystem for Existing Skill
    55в†’
    56в†’**If skills_ref tool is available:**
    57в†’
    58в†’Query the ecosystem using the skill name from the brief:
    59в†’- `skills_ref.check_ecosystem(brief.name)`
    60в†’- Enforce 5-second timeout вЂ” if the query does not return within 5 seconds, treat as no match
    61в†’- Cache results for 24 hours (if re-running same skill)
    62в†’
    63в†’**If skills_ref tool is NOT available:**
    64в†’
    65в†’Skip silently. Tool unavailability is not an error. Proceed as if no match was found.
    66в†’
    67в†’### 2. Evaluate Results
    68в†’
    69в†’**If match found (official skill exists):**
    70в†’
    71в†’Present the finding to the user:
    72в†’
    73в†’"**Ecosystem match found.**
    74в†’
    75в†’**Existing skill:** {matched_skill_name} v{matched_version}
    76в†’**Source:** {matched_source}
    77в†’**Authority:** {official/community}
    78в†’**Compatibility:** {compatibility_notes}
    79в†’
    80в†’An existing skill already covers this source. You can:
    81в†’- **[P] Proceed** вЂ” compile your own version anyway (useful for customization or different scope)
    82в†’- **[I] Install** вЂ” install the existing skill instead of compiling
    83в†’- **[A] Abort** вЂ” cancel this compilation"
    84в†’
    85в†’**If no match found:**
    86в†’
    87в†’Auto-proceed silently to next step. Do not display any message about the ecosystem check вЂ” absence of a match is the expected case.
    88в†’
    89в†’**If timeout or error:**
    90в†’
    91в†’Auto-proceed silently. Log a note in context: "Ecosystem check skipped (timeout/error) вЂ” proceeding with compilation."
    92в†’
    93в†’### 3. Menu Handling Logic
    94в†’
    95в†’**Conditional menu вЂ” only displayed if a match was found.**
    96в†’
    97в†’#### If Match Found вЂ” Present MENU OPTIONS:
    98в†’
    99в†’Display: "**Ecosystem match found вЂ” Select an Option:** [P] Proceed with compilation [I] Install existing [A] Abort"
   100в†’
   101в†’#### EXECUTION RULES:
   102в†’
   103в†’- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting menu
   104в†’- This menu ONLY appears when an ecosystem match is found
   105в†’- If no match, timeout, or tool unavailable вЂ” auto-proceed with no menu
   106в†’
   107в†’#### Menu Handling Logic:
   108в†’
   109в†’- IF P: Note user's decision to proceed despite existing skill. Immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`
   110в†’- IF I: Display: "Install the existing skill using: `[SF] Setup Forge в†’ install {matched_skill_name}`" then halt workflow. Do not proceed to extraction.
   111в†’- IF A: Display: "Compilation aborted. Return to Ferris menu to select another action." then halt workflow.
   112в†’- IF no match/timeout/error: Auto-proceed вЂ” immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`
   113в†’- IF Any other comments or queries: help user respond then redisplay the menu
   114в†’
   115в†’## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   116в†’
   117в†’ONLY WHEN the ecosystem check is complete (match evaluated, user decision made if applicable) will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for source extraction.
   118в†’
   119в†’If no match is found, this step auto-proceeds with no user interaction.
   120в†’
   121в†’---
   122в†’
   123в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   124в†’
   125в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   126в†’
   127в†’- Ecosystem check attempted (or gracefully skipped if tool unavailable)
   128в†’- 5-second timeout enforced on ecosystem queries
   129в†’- Match presented factually with clear options if found
   130в†’- No-match case auto-proceeds silently
   131в†’- User decision respected (Proceed/Install/Abort)
   132в†’- Auto-proceeded to step-03 (when no match or user chose Proceed)
   133в†’
   134в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   135в†’
   136в†’- Halting the workflow because ecosystem check failed or timed out
   137в†’- Treating tool unavailability as an error
   138в†’- Displaying "no match found" messages (absence should be silent)
   139в†’- Beginning extraction or compilation work in this step
   140в†’- Proceeding without user decision when a match IS found
   141в†’- Not enforcing the 5-second timeout
   142в†’
   143в†’**Master Rule:** This step is advisory. Tool failures and timeouts are silent skips, not errors. Only a confirmed match requires user interaction.
   144в†’

     2в†’name: 'step-03-extract'
     3в†’description: 'Tier-dependent source code extraction вЂ” AST or source reading for exports, signatures, and types'
     4в†’nextStepFile: './step-04-enrich.md'
     5в†’extractionPatternsData: '../data/extraction-patterns.md'
     6в†’---
     7в†’
     8в†’# Step 3: Extract
     9в†’
    10в†’## STEP GOAL:
    11в†’
    12в†’To extract all public exports, function signatures, type definitions, and co-import patterns from the source code using tier-appropriate tools, building a complete extraction inventory with confidence-tiered provenance citations.
    13в†’
    14в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15в†’
    16в†’### Universal Rules:
    17в†’
    18в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19в†’- рџЋЇ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    20в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22в†’
    23в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    24в†’
    25в†’- вњ… You are a skill compilation engine performing structural extraction
    26в†’- вњ… Zero hallucination tolerance вЂ” every extracted item must trace to source code
    27в†’- вњ… Confidence tier labeling is mandatory for every extraction result
    28в†’
    29в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    30в†’
    31в†’- рџЋЇ Focus ONLY on extracting exports, signatures, types from source code
    32в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to compile SKILL.md content вЂ” that's step-05
    33в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to write any output files вЂ” extraction stays in context
    34в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to include content that cannot be cited to a source location
    35в†’- вљ’пёЏ Every extracted item MUST have a provenance citation: `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` or `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]`
    36в†’
    37в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38в†’
    39в†’- рџЋЇ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    40в†’- рџ’ѕ Build extraction inventory in context вЂ” do not write files
    41в†’- рџ“– Load extraction patterns data file for tier-specific strategy
    42в†’- рџљ« If an export cannot be verified, exclude it вЂ” do not guess
    43в†’
    44в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    45в†’
    46в†’- Available: brief_data, tier, source_location, file_tree from step-01; ecosystem_status from step-02
    47в†’- Focus: Source code extraction and inventory building
    48в†’- Limits: Do NOT compile, assemble, or write any output
    49в†’- Dependencies: Source code must be accessible (resolved in step-01)
    50в†’
    51в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52в†’
    53в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54в†’
    55в†’### 1. Load Extraction Patterns
    56в†’
    57в†’Load `{extractionPatternsData}` completely. Identify the strategy for the current forge tier.
    58в†’
    59в†’### 2. Apply Scope Filters
    60в†’
    61в†’From the brief, apply scope and pattern filters:
    62в†’
    63в†’- `scope` вЂ” determines what to extract (e.g., "all public exports", specific modules)
    64в†’- `include_patterns` вЂ” file globs to include (if specified)
    65в†’- `exclude_patterns` вЂ” file globs to exclude (if specified)
    66в†’
    67в†’Build the filtered file list from the source tree resolved in step-01.
    68в†’
    69в†’### 3. Execute Tier-Dependent Extraction
    70в†’
    71в†’**Quick Tier (No AST tools):**
    72в†’
    73в†’1. Use `gh_bridge.list_tree(owner, repo, branch)` to map source structure (if remote)
    74в†’2. Identify entry points: index files, main exports, public modules
    75в†’3. Use `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, path)` to read each entry point
    76в†’4. Extract from source text: exported function names, parameter lists, return types
    77в†’5. Infer types from JSDoc, docstrings, type annotations
    78в†’6. Confidence: All results T1-low вЂ” `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]`
    79в†’
    80в†’**Forge/Deep Tier (AST available):**
    81в†’
    82в†’1. Detect language from brief or file extensions
    83в†’2. Use `ast_bridge.scan_definitions(path, language)` for each file in scope
    84в†’3. For each export: extract function name, full signature, parameter types, return type, line number
    85в†’4. Use `ast_bridge.detect_co_imports(path, libraries[])` to find integration points
    86в†’5. Build extraction rules YAML data for reproducibility
    87в†’6. Confidence: All results T1 вЂ” `[AST:{file}:L{line}]`
    88в†’
    89в†’**If AST tool is unavailable at Forge/Deep tier:**
    90в†’
    91в†’Degrade to Quick tier extraction silently. Note the degradation in context for the evidence report.
    92в†’
    93в†’**For each file вЂ” handle failures gracefully:**
    94в†’
    95в†’- If a file cannot be read: log warning, skip file, continue with remaining files
    96в†’- If AST parsing fails on a file: fall back to source reading for that file, continue
    97в†’
    98в†’### 4. Build Extraction Inventory
    99в†’
   100в†’Compile all extracted data into a structured inventory:
   101в†’
   102в†’**Per-export entry:**
   103в†’- Function/type name
   104в†’- Full signature with types
   105в†’- Parameters (name, type, required/optional)
   106в†’- Return type
   107в†’- Source file and line number
   108в†’- Provenance citation (`[AST:...]` or `[SRC:...]`)
   109в†’- Confidence tier (T1 or T1-low)
   110в†’
   111в†’**Aggregate counts:**
   112в†’- Total files scanned
   113в†’- Total exports found
   114в†’- Exports by type (functions, types/interfaces, constants)
   115в†’- Confidence breakdown (T1 count, T1-low count)
   116в†’
   117в†’**Co-import patterns (Forge/Deep only):**
   118в†’- Libraries commonly imported alongside extracted exports
   119в†’- Integration point suggestions
   120в†’
   121в†’### 5. Present Extraction Summary (Gate 2)
   122в†’
   123в†’Display the extraction findings for user confirmation:
   124в†’
   125в†’"**Extraction complete.**
   126в†’
   127в†’**Files scanned:** {file_count}
   128в†’**Exports found:** {export_count} ({function_count} functions, {type_count} types, {constant_count} constants)
   129в†’**Confidence:** {t1_count} T1 (AST-verified), {t1_low_count} T1-low (source reading)
   130в†’**Tier used:** {tier}
   131в†’**Co-import patterns:** {pattern_count} detected
   132в†’
   133в†’**Top exports:**
   134в†’{list top 10 exports with signatures}
   135в†’
   136в†’{warnings if any files skipped or degraded}
   137в†’
   138в†’Review the extraction summary above. Select an option to continue."
   139в†’
   140в†’### 6. Present MENU OPTIONS
   141в†’
   142в†’Display: "**Extraction Summary вЂ” Select an Option:** [C] Continue to compilation"
   143в†’
   144в†’#### EXECUTION RULES:
   145в†’
   146в†’- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting the extraction summary
   147в†’- This is Gate 2 вЂ” user must confirm before compilation proceeds
   148в†’- User may ask questions about the extraction results before continuing
   149в†’
   150в†’#### Menu Handling Logic:
   151в†’
   152в†’- IF C: Confirm extraction inventory is complete. Immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`
   153в†’- IF Any other comments or queries: answer questions about the extraction results, then redisplay the menu
   154в†’
   155в†’## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   156в†’
   157в†’ONLY WHEN the extraction inventory is built with provenance citations and the user has confirmed the extraction summary will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for QMD enrichment.
   158в†’
   159в†’---
   160в†’
   161в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   162в†’
   163в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   164в†’
   165в†’- Extraction patterns loaded and tier-appropriate strategy applied
   166в†’- Scope filters applied from brief
   167в†’- All accessible source files in scope scanned
   168в†’- Every extracted item has a provenance citation with confidence tier
   169в†’- Extraction inventory built with aggregate counts
   170в†’- Graceful degradation if AST tools unavailable
   171в†’- Gate 2 summary presented with export counts and confidence breakdown
   172в†’- User confirmed before proceeding
   173в†’
   174в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   175в†’
   176в†’- Including exports without provenance citations
   177в†’- Guessing or hallucinating function signatures not in source
   178в†’- Not applying scope/include/exclude filters from the brief
   179в†’- Halting on individual file read failures instead of skipping
   180в†’- Not presenting Gate 2 summary for user confirmation
   181в†’- Beginning compilation or SKILL.md assembly in this step
   182в†’- Not reporting confidence tier breakdown
   183в†’
   184в†’**Master Rule:** Zero hallucination вЂ” every extraction must trace to source code. Uncitable content is excluded, not guessed.
   185в†’

     2в†’name: 'step-04-enrich'

     5в†’---
     6в†’
     7в†’# Step 4: Enrich
     8в†’
     9в†’## STEP GOAL:
    10в†’
    11в†’To enrich the extraction inventory with temporal context from QMD knowledge searches вЂ” issues, PRs, changelogs, and migration notes that add T2-confidence annotations to extracted functions. Deep tier only; Quick and Forge tiers skip this step entirely.
    12в†’
    13в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    14в†’
    15в†’### Universal Rules:
    16в†’
    17в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    18в†’- рџЋЇ ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    19в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21в†’
    22в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    23в†’
    24в†’- вњ… You are a skill compilation engine performing knowledge enrichment
    25в†’- вњ… QMD enrichment adds temporal depth вЂ” it never replaces AST extraction
    26в†’- вњ… Enrichment failure is graceful degradation, not an error
    27в†’
    28в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    29в†’
    30в†’- рџЋЇ Focus ONLY on QMD searches to annotate extracted functions
    31в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to modify extraction results вЂ” enrichment is additive only
    32в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to begin compilation вЂ” that's step-05
    33в†’- рџ’¬ QMD failures degrade gracefully вЂ” continue without enrichment
    34в†’- вЏ±пёЏ Quick and Forge tiers: skip this step entirely, auto-proceed
    35в†’
    36в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37в†’
    38в†’- рџЋЇ Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    39в†’- рџ’ѕ Enrichment annotations are added to extraction inventory in context
    40в†’- рџ“– QMD results become T2-confidence annotations
    41в†’- рџљ« Never replace T1 extraction data with QMD findings
    42в†’
    43в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44в†’
    45в†’- Available: extraction_inventory from step-03, brief_data, tier from step-01
    46в†’- Focus: Adding temporal context to extracted functions
    47в†’- Limits: Do NOT modify extraction results, only annotate them
    48в†’- Dependencies: Extraction inventory must exist from step-03
    49в†’
    50в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    51в†’
    52в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    53в†’
    54в†’### 1. Check Tier Eligibility
    55в†’
    56в†’**If tier is Quick or Forge:**
    57в†’
    58в†’Auto-proceed silently. Display no message. Immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
    59в†’
    60в†’**If tier is Deep:**
    61в†’
    62в†’Continue to step 2.
    63в†’
    64в†’### 2. QMD Enrichment Searches (Deep Tier Only)
    65в†’
    66в†’For each major exported function in the extraction inventory, search QMD collections for temporal context:
    67в†’
    68в†’**Search targets per function:**
    69в†’
    70в†’1. **Issues/PRs:** `qmd_bridge.search("{function_name}")` вЂ” find related discussions, bug reports, feature requests
    71в†’2. **Changelog entries:** `qmd_bridge.search("{function_name} changelog")` вЂ” find version history, breaking changes
    72в†’3. **Migration notes:** `qmd_bridge.vector_search("{function_name} migration deprecated")` вЂ” find deprecation or migration context
    73в†’
    74в†’**For each QMD result:**
    75в†’
    76в†’- Create a T2 annotation: `[QMD:{collection}:{doc}]`
    77в†’- Classify temporal relevance:
    78в†’  - **T2-past:** Historical context (why it was built, what it replaced)
    79в†’  - **T2-future:** Forward-looking context (planned changes, deprecation warnings)
    80в†’
    81в†’**Handling QMD failures:**
    82в†’
    83в†’- If qmd_bridge is unavailable: skip all enrichment, note in context
    84в†’- If individual search fails: skip that function's enrichment, continue with others
    85в†’- If QMD returns no results for a function: no annotation added (absence is normal)
    86в†’
    87в†’### 3. Annotate Extraction Inventory
    88в†’
    89в†’Add enrichment annotations to the extraction inventory without modifying extraction data:
    90в†’
    91в†’**Per-function enrichment (if found):**
    92в†’- Related issues/PRs with summary
    93в†’- Changelog history (version changes, breaking changes)
    94в†’- Migration/deprecation context
    95в†’- T2 provenance citation for each annotation
    96в†’
    97в†’**Enrichment summary counts:**
    98в†’- Functions enriched: {count} of {total}
    99в†’- T2 annotations added: {count}
   100в†’- T2-past annotations: {count}
   101в†’- T2-future annotations: {count}
   102в†’
   103в†’### 4. Report Enrichment (Deep Tier Only)
   104в†’
   105в†’Display brief enrichment summary:
   106в†’
   107в†’"**Enrichment complete.**
   108в†’
   109в†’**Functions enriched:** {enriched_count} of {total_count}
   110в†’**T2 annotations:** {t2_count} ({t2_past} historical, {t2_future} forward-looking)
   111в†’
   112в†’Proceeding to compilation..."
   113в†’
   114в†’### 5. Menu Handling Logic
   115в†’
   116в†’**Auto-proceed step вЂ” no user interaction.**
   117в†’
   118в†’After enrichment is complete (or skipped for non-Deep tiers), immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   119в†’
   120в†’#### EXECUTION RULES:
   121в†’
   122в†’- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   123в†’- Quick/Forge tiers skip directly to next step with no output
   124в†’- Deep tier displays brief enrichment summary then auto-proceeds
   125в†’- QMD failures do not halt вЂ” degrade and proceed
   126в†’
   127в†’## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   128в†’
   129в†’ONLY WHEN enrichment is complete (Deep tier) or the step is skipped (Quick/Forge) will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for SKILL.md compilation.

   132в†’
   133в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   134в†’
   135в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   136в†’
   137в†’- Quick/Forge tiers: skipped silently with no output, auto-proceeded
   138в†’- Deep tier: QMD searches performed per extracted function
   139в†’- T2 annotations added with proper provenance citations
   140в†’- Extraction data unmodified вЂ” enrichment is additive only
   141в†’- QMD failures handled gracefully (skip and continue)
   142в†’- Auto-proceeded to step-05
   143в†’
   144в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   145в†’

   147в†’- Modifying T1 extraction results with QMD data
   148в†’- Displaying skip messages for Quick/Forge tiers (should be silent)
   149в†’- Beginning compilation or SKILL.md assembly in this step
   150в†’- Not labeling QMD annotations as T2 confidence
   151в†’
   152в†’**Master Rule:** Enrichment is additive and optional. QMD adds temporal depth but never replaces structural extraction. Failures degrade gracefully.

    66в†’
    67в†’**If forge-tier.yaml exists:**
    68в†’
    69в†’Extract:
    70в†’- `forge_tier` вЂ” Quick, Forge, or Deep
    71в†’- `available_tools` вЂ” list of detected tools (gh_bridge, ast_bridge, qmd_bridge, skills_ref)
    72в†’- `project_root` вЂ” project root path
    73в†’
    74в†’Check for `tier_override` in preferences.yaml if it exists.
    75в†’
    76в†’### 2. Validate Available Tools
    77в†’
    78в†’**Required for all tiers:**
    79в†’- File I/O capability (read project files)
    80в†’
    81в†’**Tier-dependent tools:**
    82в†’- **Quick:** gh_bridge (source reading) вЂ” graceful degradation to local file reading if unavailable
    83в†’- **Forge:** ast_bridge (ast-grep structural analysis) вЂ” required for Forge tier
    84в†’- **Deep:** qmd_bridge (QMD temporal enrichment) вЂ” required for Deep tier
    85в†’
    86в†’Report tool availability. If a tier-required tool is missing, downgrade tier and note:
    87в†’
    88в†’"**Tier adjusted:** {original_tier} в†’ {adjusted_tier} вЂ” {missing_tool} unavailable."
    89в†’
    90в†’### 3. Accept Optional Inputs
    91в†’
    92в†’Check if the user provided:
    93в†’
    94в†’**Explicit dependency list:**
    95в†’- If provided, store as `explicit_deps` and skip auto-detection in step 02
    96в†’- Format: comma-separated library names or a file path
    97в†’
    98в†’**Scope overrides:**
    99в†’- If provided, store as `scope_overrides` for use in step 03
   100в†’- Format: `library_name: include|exclude`
   101в†’
   102в†’If no optional inputs provided, auto-detection will be used.
   103в†’
   104в†’### 4. Display Initialization Summary
   105в†’
   106в†’"**Stack Skill Forge initialized.**
   107в†’
   108в†’**Project:** {project_name}
   109в†’**Forge Tier:** {tier_description}
   110в†’- Quick: Source reading and import counting
   111в†’- Forge: + AST-backed structural analysis
   112в†’- Deep: + Temporal integration evolution
   113в†’
   114в†’**Available Tools:** {tool_list}
   115в†’**Input Mode:** {auto-detect | explicit dependency list}
   116в†’
   117в†’**Proceeding to manifest detection...**"
   118в†’
   119в†’### 5. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   120в†’
   121в†’Load, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   122в†’
   123в†’---
   124в†’
   125в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   126в†’
   127в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   128в†’
   129в†’- Forge tier loaded and validated
   130в†’- Tool availability confirmed with tier adjustment if needed
   131в†’- Optional inputs accepted and stored
   132в†’- Initialization summary displayed with positive capability framing
   133в†’- Auto-proceeded to step 02
   134в†’
   135в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   136в†’
   137в†’- Proceeding without forge-tier.yaml
   138в†’- Not reporting tier adjustments when tools missing
   139в†’- Starting manifest detection in this step
   140в†’- Displaying A/P menu (this is auto-proceed)
   141в†’
   142в†’**Master Rule:** This step validates prerequisites and initializes state. No analysis, no detection, no extraction.
   143в†’

1в†’---
     2в†’name: 'step-02-detect-manifests'
     3в†’description: 'Scan project for dependency manifests and extract raw dependency list'
     4в†’
     5в†’nextStepFile: './step-03-rank-and-confirm.md'
     6в†’manifestPatterns: '../data/manifest-patterns.md'
     7в†’---
     8в†’
     9в†’# Step 2: Detect Manifests
    10в†’
    11в†’## STEP GOAL:
    12в†’
    13в†’Scan the project root for dependency manifest files, parse each to extract dependency names and versions, and produce a raw dependency list for ranking.
    14в†’
    15в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16в†’
    17в†’### Universal Rules:
    18в†’
    19в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22в†’
    23в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    24в†’
    25в†’- вњ… You are a dependency analyst operating in Ferris Architect mode
    26в†’- вњ… Prescriptive precision вЂ” report exactly what was found, nothing assumed
    27в†’- вњ… Zero hallucination вЂ” only list dependencies actually present in manifest files
    28в†’
    29в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    30в†’
    31в†’- рџЋЇ Focus ONLY on finding and parsing manifest files
    32в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to count imports or rank dependencies вЂ” that is step 03
    33в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to extract library documentation вЂ” that is step 04
    34в†’- рџ’¬ If explicit dependency list was provided in step 01, use it and skip detection
    35в†’
    36в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37в†’
    38в†’- рџЋЇ Load manifest-patterns.md for detection rules
    39в†’- рџ’ѕ Store manifests found and raw dependency list as workflow state
    40в†’- рџ“– Auto-proceed to step 03 after successful detection
    41в†’- рџљ« HALT if no manifests found and no explicit list provided
    42в†’
    43в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44в†’
    45в†’- From step 01: forge tier, available tools, optional explicit_deps, project_root
    46в†’- This step produces: manifests[], raw_dependencies[]
    47в†’- Does NOT rank, filter, or analyze вЂ” just detect and parse
    48в†’
    49в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50в†’
    51в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52в†’
    53в†’### 1. Check for Explicit Dependency List
    54в†’
    55в†’**If `explicit_deps` was provided in step 01:**
    56в†’
    57в†’"**Using provided dependency list.** Skipping manifest auto-detection.
    58в†’
    59в†’**Dependencies:** {explicit_deps_count} libraries provided"
    60в†’
    61в†’Store the explicit list as `raw_dependencies` and skip to section 4.
    62в†’
    63в†’**If no explicit list:** Continue to section 2.
    64в†’
    65в†’### 2. Scan for Manifest Files
    66в†’
    67в†’Load `{manifestPatterns}` for supported ecosystem detection patterns.
    68в†’
    69в†’Scan the project root (depth 0-1) for manifest files:
    70в†’
    71в†’- Search for each supported manifest filename
    72в†’- Record: file path, ecosystem type, file size
    73в†’- Note any unusual structures (monorepo with multiple manifests, workspace configurations)
    74в†’
    75в†’**If no manifest files found:**
    76в†’
    77в†’"**No dependency manifests detected** in the project root.
    78в†’
    79в†’Searched for: package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, go.mod, pom.xml, build.gradle, Gemfile, composer.json, *.csproj
    80в†’
    81в†’**Options:**
    82в†’1. Provide an explicit dependency list
    83в†’2. Specify a different project root path
    84в†’3. Cancel workflow
    85в†’
    86в†’**Halting вЂ” please provide input.**"
    87в†’
    88в†’STOP вЂ” wait for user response.
    89в†’
    90в†’### 3. Parse Each Manifest
    91в†’
    92в†’For each discovered manifest file:
    93в†’
    94в†’1. Read the file contents
    95в†’2. Extract dependency names and version constraints using ecosystem-specific parsing:
    96в†’   - JSON manifests: parse dependencies/devDependencies keys
    97в†’   - TOML manifests: parse [dependencies] sections
    98в†’   - Text manifests: parse line-by-line (name==version or name>=version)
    99в†’   - XML manifests: parse dependency elements
   100в†’   - Gradle: parse implementation/api/compile declarations
   101в†’3. Categorize: runtime vs dev-only
   102в†’4. Normalize dependency names across ecosystems
   103в†’
   104в†’Deduplicate dependencies found across multiple manifests.
   105в†’
   106в†’### 4. Display Detection Summary
   107в†’
   108в†’"**Manifest detection complete.**
   109в†’
   110в†’**Manifests found:** {count}
   111в†’{For each manifest:}
   112в†’- `{file_path}` ({ecosystem}) вЂ” {dep_count} dependencies
   113в†’
   114в†’**Total unique dependencies:** {total_count}
   115в†’- Runtime: {runtime_count}
   116в†’- Dev-only: {dev_count}
   117в†’
   118в†’**Proceeding to dependency ranking...**"
   119в†’
   120в†’### 5. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   121в†’
   122в†’Load, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   123в†’
   124в†’---
   125в†’
   126в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   127в†’
   128в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   129в†’
   130в†’- All manifest files in project root detected
   131в†’- Each manifest parsed with dependency names extracted
   132в†’- Runtime vs dev-only categorization applied
   133в†’- Deduplication across multiple manifests completed
   134в†’- Detection summary displayed with accurate counts
   135в†’- Auto-proceeded to step 03
   136в†’
   137в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   138в†’
   139в†’- Missing manifest files that exist in the project
   140в†’- Inventing dependencies not in manifest files
   141в†’- Starting to rank or analyze dependencies (step 03's job)
   142в†’- Not halting when no manifests found
   143в†’- Displaying A/P menu (this is auto-proceed)
   144в†’
   145в†’**Master Rule:** Detect and parse only. No ranking, no filtering, no analysis.
   146в†’

1в†’# Skill Template вЂ” Quick Skill Output
     2в†’
     3в†’## SKILL.md Section Structure
     4в†’
     5в†’The following sections should be populated in the generated SKILL.md. Best-effort вЂ” not all sections will have data for every skill.
     6в†’
     7в†’### Required Sections
     8в†’
     9в†’```markdown
    10в†’---
    11в†’name: {skill_name}
    12в†’description: >
    13в†’  {README-derived description, trigger-optimized for agent discovery.
    14в†’  Include what the package does and when to use it.
    15в†’  Mention what NOT to use it for if applicable.}
    16в†’---
    17в†’
    18в†’# {skill_name}
    19в†’
    20в†’## Overview
    21в†’- **Package:** {package_name}
    22в†’- **Repository:** {repo_url}
    23в†’- **Language:** {language}
    24в†’- **Source Authority:** community
    25в†’- **Generated:** {date}
    26в†’
    27в†’## Description
    28в†’{README-derived description of what the package does}
    29в†’
    30в†’## Key Exports
    31в†’{List of public exports with brief descriptions}
    32в†’
    33в†’## Usage Patterns
    34в†’{Common usage patterns extracted from README examples}
    35в†’```
    36в†’
    37в†’### Optional Sections (include when data available)
    38в†’
    39в†’```markdown
    40в†’## Configuration
    41в†’{Configuration options if found in source}
    42в†’
    43в†’## Dependencies
    44в†’{Key dependencies from manifest file}
    45в†’
    46в†’## Notes
    47в†’{Any caveats, limitations, or observations about the extraction}
    48в†’```
    49в†’
    50в†’## context-snippet.md Format (ADR-L)
    51в†’
    52в†’Two-line format targeting ~30 tokens per skill:
    53в†’
    54в†’```markdown
    55в†’{skill_name}: {top-5 exports as comma-separated list}
    56в†’  в†’ {skills_output_folder}/{skill_name}/SKILL.md
    57в†’```
    58в†’
    59в†’## metadata.json Format
    60в†’
    61в†’```json
    62в†’{
    63в†’  "name": "{skill_name}",
    64в†’  "version": "0.1.0",
    65в†’  "source_authority": "community",
    66в†’  "source_repo": "{repo_url}",
    67в†’  "source_package": "{package_name}",
    68в†’  "language": "{language}",
    69в†’  "generated_by": "quick-skill",
    70в†’  "generated_date": "{date}",
    71в†’  "exports_count": {number},

    73в†’}
    74в†’```
    75в†’

1в†’# SKILL.md Section Structure
     2в†’
     3в†’## agentskills.io Compliant Format
     4в†’
     5в†’### Frontmatter (Required вЂ” agentskills.io Compliance)
     6в†’
     7в†’```yaml
     8в†’---
     9в†’name: {skill-name}
    10в†’description: >
    11в†’  {Trigger-optimized description of what the skill does and when to use it.
    12в†’  Include specific keywords for agent discovery.
    13в†’  Mention what NOT to use it for if applicable.
    14в†’  1-1024 characters.}
    15в†’---
    16в†’```
    17в†’
    18в†’**Frontmatter rules (agentskills.io specification):**
    19в†’
    20в†’- `name`: 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens only, must match parent directory name
    21в†’- `description`: 1-1024 characters, trigger-optimized for agent matching
    22в†’- Only 6 fields permitted: `name`, `description`, `license`, `compatibility`, `metadata`, `allowed-tools`
    23в†’- `version` and `author` belong in metadata.json, NOT in frontmatter
    24в†’
    25в†’### Section Order
    26в†’
    27в†’1. **Overview** вЂ” What this skill provides, source repo, version, tier used
    28в†’2. **Quick Start** вЂ” Most common 3-5 functions with minimal examples
    29в†’3. **API Reference** вЂ” Grouped by module/file, each function with:
    30в†’   - Signature with types
    31в†’   - Parameters table
    32в†’   - Return type
    33в†’   - Usage example (from source tests/docs if available)
    34в†’   - Provenance citation: `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` or `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]`
    35в†’4. **Type Definitions** вЂ” Exported types, interfaces, enums
    36в†’5. **Integration Patterns** вЂ” Co-import patterns detected by ast_bridge (Forge/Deep only)
    37в†’6. **Manual Sections** вЂ” Seeded with `<!-- [MANUAL] -->` markers for update-skill
    38в†’
    39в†’### Provenance Citation Format
    40в†’
    41в†’| Tier | Format | Example |
    42в†’|------|--------|---------|
    43в†’| T1 (AST) | `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` | `[AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
    44в†’| T1-low (Source) | `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]` | `[SRC:src/auth/index.ts:L42]` |
    45в†’| T2 (QMD) | `[QMD:{collection}:{doc}]` | `[QMD:project:CHANGELOG.md]` |
    46в†’| T3 (External) | `[EXT:{url}]` | `[EXT:docs.example.com/api]` |
    47в†’
    48в†’### [MANUAL] Section Markers
    49в†’
    50в†’Seed empty manual sections for future update-skill compatibility:
    51в†’
    52в†’```markdown
    53в†’<!-- [MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    54в†’<!-- Add custom notes here. This section is preserved during skill updates. -->
    55в†’<!-- [/MANUAL:additional-notes] -->
    56в†’```
    57в†’
    58в†’Place after Quick Start and after API Reference sections.
    59в†’
    60в†’---
    61в†’
    62в†’## context-snippet.md Format
    63в†’
    64в†’Compressed 2-line-per-skill format for CLAUDE.md managed section:
    65в†’
    66в†’```markdown
    67в†’{skill-name} -> skills/{skill-name}/
    68в†’  exports: {comma-separated top 10 function names}
    69в†’```
    70в†’
    71в†’---
    72в†’
    73в†’## metadata.json Structure
    74в†’
    75в†’```json
    76в†’{
    77в†’  "name": "{skill-name}",
    78в†’  "version": "{source-version}",
    79в†’  "skill_type": "individual",
    80в†’  "source_authority": "{official|community|internal}",
    81в†’  "source_repo": "{github-url}",
    82в†’  "source_commit": "{commit-hash}",
    83в†’  "forge_tier": "{quick|forge|deep}",
    84в†’  "spec_version": "1.3",
    85в†’  "generated_at": "{ISO-8601}",
    86в†’  "tool_versions": {
    87в†’    "ast_grep": "{version-or-null}",
    88в†’    "qmd": "{version-or-null}",
    89в†’    "skf": "1.0.0"
    90в†’  },
    91в†’  "stats": {
    92в†’    "exports_documented": 0,
    93в†’    "exports_total": 0,
    94в†’    "coverage": 0.0,
    95в†’    "confidence_t1": 0,
    96в†’    "confidence_t2": 0,
    97в†’    "confidence_t3": 0
    98в†’  },
    99в†’  "dependencies": [],
   100в†’  "compatibility": "{semver-range}"
   101в†’}
   102в†’```
   103в†’
   104в†’---
   105в†’
   106в†’## references/ Directory Structure
   107в†’
   108в†’One file per major function group or type:
   109в†’
   110в†’```
   111в†’references/
   112в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ {function-group-a}.md    вЂ” Detailed reference with full examples
   113в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ {function-group-b}.md    вЂ” Detailed reference with full examples
   114в†’в””в”Ђв”Ђ {type-name}.md           вЂ” Type definition details
   115в†’```
   116в†’
   117в†’Each reference file includes:
   118в†’- Full function signatures
   119в†’- Detailed parameter descriptions
   120в†’- Return value details
   121в†’- Complete usage examples
   122в†’- Related functions cross-references
   123в†’- Temporal annotations (Deep tier: T2-past, T2-future)
   124в†’
   125в†’---
   126в†’
   127в†’## provenance-map.json Structure
   128в†’

   130в†’{
   131в†’  "skill_name": "{name}",
   132в†’  "source_repo": "{url}",

   134в†’  "generated_at": "{ISO-8601}",
   135в†’  "entries": [

   146в†’```
   147в†’
   148в†’---

   150в†’## evidence-report.md Structure

   154в†’
   155в†’**Generated:** {date}

     7в†’
     8в†’# Step 3: Rank and Confirm Scope
     9в†’
    10в†’## STEP GOAL:
    11в†’
    12в†’Count import frequency for each dependency across the codebase, rank by usage, and present for user confirmation of which libraries to include in the stack skill.
    13в†’
    14в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15в†’
    16в†’### Universal Rules:
    17в†’
    18в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21в†’
    22в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    23в†’
    24в†’- вњ… You are a dependency analyst operating in Ferris Architect mode
    25в†’- вњ… Prescriptive precision вЂ” import counts must reflect actual code
    26в†’- вњ… User has final authority on scope вЂ” present data, let them decide
    27в†’
    28в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    29в†’
    30в†’- рџЋЇ Focus on counting imports, ranking, and getting user confirmation
    31в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to extract library documentation вЂ” that is step 04
    32в†’- рџ’¬ Present data clearly so user can make informed scope decisions
    33в†’- рџЋЇ Use subprocess (Pattern 1 grep) for import counting when available
    34в†’
    35в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36в†’
    37в†’- рџЋЇ Count imports per library across the codebase
    38в†’- рџ’ѕ Store confirmed_dependencies as workflow state
    39в†’- рџ“– Wait for user confirmation before proceeding
    40в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to proceed without explicit user scope confirmation
    41в†’
    42в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    43в†’
    44в†’- From step 02: raw_dependencies[] with ecosystem info
    45в†’- This step produces: confirmed_dependencies[] (user-approved scope)
    46в†’- User interaction: Gate 1 вЂ” scope confirmation required
    47в†’- Apply scope_overrides from step 01 if provided
    48в†’
    49в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50в†’
    51в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52в†’
    53в†’### 1. Count Import Frequency
    54в†’
    55в†’For each dependency in `raw_dependencies`:
    56в†’
    57в†’**Launch a subprocess** that runs grep across all source files in the project to count import statements for each library. Return only the counts, not file contents.
    58в†’
    59в†’Use ecosystem-appropriate import patterns:
    60в†’- JavaScript/TypeScript: `import .* from ['"]library`, `require\(['"]library`
    61в†’- Python: `import library`, `from library import`
    62в†’- Rust: `use library::`, `extern crate library`
    63в†’- Go: `"library"` in import blocks
    64в†’- (Match patterns from manifest-patterns.md)
    65в†’
    66в†’**Subprocess returns:** `{library_name: import_count, files: [file_paths]}` for each dependency.
    67в†’
    68в†’**If subprocess unavailable:** Perform grep operations in main thread sequentially.
    69в†’
    70в†’Exclude from counting:
    71в†’- Test files (*/test/*, *_test.*, *.spec.*, *.test.*)
    72в†’- Config files (*.config.*, .eslintrc, etc.)
    73в†’- Build artifacts (dist/, build/, node_modules/, target/, __pycache__/)
    74в†’
    75в†’### 2. Rank and Filter
    76в†’
    77в†’Sort dependencies by import count (descending).
    78в†’
    79в†’Apply filtering:
    80в†’- **Include by default:** Libraries with 2+ import files
    81в†’- **Flag as trivial:** Libraries with 0-1 import files
    82в†’- **Apply scope_overrides** from step 01 if provided (force include/exclude)
    83в†’
    84в†’### 3. Present Ranked List
    85в†’
    86в†’"**Dependency ranking complete.** Here are your project's libraries ranked by usage:
    87в†’
    88в†’| # | Library | Imports | Files | Category |
    89в†’|---|---------|---------|-------|----------|
    90в†’| 1 | {name} | {count} | {file_count} | runtime |
    91в†’| 2 | {name} | {count} | {file_count} | runtime |
    92в†’| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
    93в†’
    94в†’**Below threshold** (0-1 imports вЂ” excluded by default):
    95в†’| Library | Imports | Category |
    96в†’|---------|---------|----------|
    97в†’| {name} | {count} | {category} |
    98в†’
    99в†’**Total:** {total} dependencies detected, {above_threshold} recommended for inclusion
   100в†’
   101в†’---
   102в†’
   103в†’**Please confirm your scope:**
   104в†’- Type **C** to accept the recommended scope (all above-threshold libraries)
   105в†’- Type library names to **add** from the below-threshold list
   106в†’- Type **-library_name** to **exclude** a recommended library
   107в†’- Type a custom list to override entirely"
   108в†’
   109в†’### 4. Process User Response
   110в†’
   111в†’**If C (accept recommended):**
   112в†’Store all above-threshold libraries as `confirmed_dependencies`.
   113в†’
   114в†’**If modifications requested:**
   115в†’Apply additions/exclusions, display updated list, and ask for final confirmation.
   116в†’
   117в†’**If custom list provided:**
   118в†’Use the custom list as `confirmed_dependencies`.
   119в†’
   120в†’Display final confirmation:
   121в†’
   122в†’"**Scope confirmed:** {count} libraries selected for stack skill extraction.
   123в†’
   124в†’{List confirmed libraries}
   125в†’
   126в†’**Proceeding to parallel extraction...**"
   127в†’
   128в†’### 5. Present MENU OPTIONS
   129в†’
   130в†’Display: **Select:** [C] Continue to Extraction
   131в†’
   132в†’#### EXECUTION RULES:
   133в†’
   134в†’- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting scope
   135в†’- ONLY proceed to next step when user confirms scope and selects 'C'
   136в†’
   137в†’#### Menu Handling Logic:
   138в†’
   139в†’- IF C: Store confirmed_dependencies, then load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   140в†’- IF Any other: Process as scope modification, redisplay updated list, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#5-present-menu-options)
   141в†’
   142в†’---
   143в†’
   144в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   145в†’
   146в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   147в†’
   148в†’- Import counts reflect actual codebase usage (not guesses)
   149в†’- Dependencies ranked by import frequency
   150в†’- Trivial dependencies filtered below threshold
   151в†’- User explicitly confirmed scope
   152в†’- confirmed_dependencies stored for step 04
   153в†’
   154в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   155в†’
   156в†’- Fabricating import counts without grepping
   157в†’- Proceeding without user scope confirmation
   158в†’- Including test/config files in import counts
   159в†’- Starting library extraction (step 04's job)
   160в†’
   161в†’**Master Rule:** Present data, let user decide. No extraction until scope is confirmed.
   162в†’

     7в†’
     8в†’# Step 4: Parallel Library Extraction
     9в†’
    10в†’## STEP GOAL:
    11в†’
    12в†’For each confirmed dependency, extract key exports, usage patterns, and API surface documentation using tier-dependent tools in parallel.
    13в†’
    14в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15в†’
    16в†’### Universal Rules:
    17в†’
    18в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    20в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    21в†’
    22в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    23в†’
    24в†’- вњ… You are a dependency analyst operating in Ferris Architect mode
    25в†’- вњ… Zero hallucination вЂ” only extract what exists in actual source code
    26в†’- вњ… Every export and pattern must cite actual file:line references
    27в†’
    28в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    29в†’
    30в†’- рџЋЇ Extract per-library using Pattern 4 (parallel subprocess) when available
    31в†’- рџ’¬ Each subprocess returns structured extraction, not raw file contents
    32в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to analyze cross-library integrations вЂ” that is step 05
    33в†’- вљ™пёЏ If parallel subprocess unavailable, extract libraries sequentially in main thread
    34в†’
    35в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    36в†’
    37в†’- рџЋЇ Launch parallel extraction per confirmed library
    38в†’- рџ’ѕ Store per_library_extractions with confidence tier labels
    39в†’- рџ“– Report extraction summary before auto-proceeding
    40в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to proceed if zero libraries successfully extracted
    41в†’
    42в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    43в†’
    44в†’- From step 03: confirmed_dependencies[] (user-approved scope)
    45в†’- From step 01: forge_tier, available_tools
    46в†’- This step produces: per_library_extractions[] per library
    47в†’- Each extraction includes: exports, patterns, confidence tier
    48в†’
    49в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    50в†’
    51в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    52в†’
    53в†’### 1. Prepare Extraction Plan
    54в†’
    55в†’For each library in `confirmed_dependencies`, determine extraction strategy based on forge tier:
    56в†’
    57в†’**Quick Tier:**
    58в†’- Read source files that import the library
    59в†’- Extract usage patterns from import statements and function calls
    60в†’- Identify key exports used in this project
    61в†’- Confidence: T1-low (source reading inference)
    62в†’
    63в†’**Forge Tier (adds to Quick):**
    64в†’- Use ast_bridge to analyze structural exports from library source
    65в†’- Extract function signatures, type definitions, class hierarchies
    66в†’- Map parameter types and return types
    67в†’- Confidence: T1 (AST-verified structural extraction)
    68в†’
    69в†’**Deep Tier (adds to Forge):**
    70в†’- Use qmd_bridge for temporal usage evolution
    71в†’- Identify deprecated patterns and migration paths
    72в†’- Track API changes across project history
    73в†’- Confidence: T2 (QMD-enriched temporal context)
    74в†’
    75в†’### 2. Launch Parallel Extraction
    76в†’
    77в†’**Launch subprocesses in parallel** (max_parallel_generation) вЂ” one per confirmed library:
    78в†’
    79в†’Each subprocess:
    80в†’1. Reads all files importing the library (from step 03 file lists)
    81в†’2. Extracts key exports used in this project (functions, classes, types, constants)
    82в†’3. Identifies usage patterns (initialization, configuration, common call patterns)
    83в†’4. Labels confidence tier based on extraction method
    84в†’5. Returns structured extraction to parent:
    85в†’
    86в†’```
    87в†’{
    88в†’  library: "name",
    89в†’  version: "from_manifest",
    90в†’  exports_found: ["fn1", "fn2", "Type1"],
    91в†’  usage_patterns: ["pattern description with file:line"],
    92в†’  confidence: "T1|T1-low|T2",
    93в†’  files_analyzed: count,
    94в†’  warnings: []
    95в†’}
    96в†’```
    97в†’
    98в†’**If parallel subprocess unavailable:** Process libraries sequentially in main thread. Report progress after each library.
    99в†’
   100в†’### 3. Handle Extraction Failures
   101в†’
   102в†’For each library extraction:
   103в†’
   104в†’**Success:** Store extraction result.
   105в†’
   106в†’**Partial failure:** Store partial result with warnings, continue with other libraries.
   107в†’
   108в†’**Complete failure:** Log failure reason, exclude from stack skill, note in report.
   109в†’
   110в†’"**Warning:** Extraction failed for {library}: {reason}. Excluding from stack skill."
   111в†’
   112в†’**If ALL extractions fail:** HALT вЂ” cannot produce meaningful stack skill.
   113в†’
   114в†’### 4. Display Extraction Summary
   115в†’
   116в†’"**Library extraction complete.**
   117в†’
   118в†’| Library | Exports | Patterns | Confidence | Status |
   119в†’|---------|---------|----------|------------|--------|
   120в†’| {name} | {count} | {count} | {tier} | вњ“ |
   121в†’| {name} | {count} | {count} | {tier} | вњ“ |
   122в†’| {name} | вЂ” | вЂ” | вЂ” | вљ  partial |
   123в†’
   124в†’**Results:** {success_count}/{total_count} libraries extracted
   125в†’**Confidence distribution:** T1: {count}, T1-low: {count}, T2: {count}
   126в†’{If warnings:} **Warnings:** {warning_count} issues noted
   127в†’
   128в†’**Proceeding to integration detection...**"
   129в†’
   130в†’### 5. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   131в†’
   132в†’Load, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   133в†’
   134в†’---
   135в†’
   136в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   137в†’
   138в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   139в†’
   140в†’- All confirmed libraries have extraction attempted
   141в†’- Extractions cite actual file:line references
   142в†’- Confidence tiers correctly assigned per extraction method
   143в†’- Partial failures handled gracefully (skip library, continue)
   144в†’- Extraction summary displayed with accurate counts
   145в†’- Auto-proceeded to step 05
   146в†’
   147в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   148в†’
   149в†’- Fabricating exports not found in source code
   150в†’- Not assigning confidence tiers to extractions
   151в†’- Halting on single library failure (should degrade gracefully)
   152в†’- Analyzing cross-library integrations (step 05's job)
   153в†’- Returning raw file contents instead of structured extractions
   154в†’
   155в†’**Master Rule:** Extract per-library only. Every export must trace to actual code. Degrade gracefully on individual failures.
   156в†’

1в†’---
     2в†’name: 'step-05-detect-integrations'
     3в†’description: 'Detect co-import patterns and integration points between confirmed libraries'
     4в†’
     5в†’nextStepFile: './step-06-compile-stack.md'
     6в†’integrationPatterns: '../data/integration-patterns.md'
     7в†’---
     8в†’
     9в†’# Step 5: Detect Integrations
    10в†’
    11в†’## STEP GOAL:
    12в†’
    13в†’Analyze co-import patterns between confirmed libraries to identify integration points вЂ” where and how libraries connect in this specific codebase.
    14в†’
    15в†’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16в†’
    17в†’### Universal Rules:
    18в†’
    19в†’- рџ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20в†’- вљ™пёЏ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21в†’- вњ… YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22в†’
    23в†’### Role Reinforcement:
    24в†’
    25в†’- вњ… You are an integration architect operating in Ferris Architect mode
    26в†’- вњ… Zero hallucination вЂ” only document integrations evidenced in actual code
    27в†’- вњ… Every integration pattern must cite co-import files with file:line references
    28в†’
    29в†’### Step-Specific Rules:
    30в†’
    31в†’- рџЋЇ Focus on detecting cross-library patterns using Pattern 1 (grep) subprocess
    32в†’- рџљ« FORBIDDEN to compile SKILL.md вЂ” that is step 06
    33в†’- рџ’¬ Integration detection is the core differentiator of stack skills vs individual skills
    34в†’- вљ™пёЏ If subprocess unavailable, perform grep operations in main thread
    35в†’
    36в†’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    37в†’
    38в†’- рџЋЇ Load integration-patterns.md for detection rules
    39в†’- рџ’ѕ Store integration_graph as workflow state
    40в†’- рџ“– Auto-proceed to step 06 after detection complete
    41в†’- рџљ« Graceful handling if no integrations detected
    42в†’
    43в†’## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    44в†’
    45в†’- From step 03: confirmed_dependencies[] with file lists per library
    46в†’- From step 04: per_library_extractions[] with exports and patterns
    47в†’- This step produces: integration_graph {pairs[], types[], files[]}
    48в†’- This is the VALUE-ADD step вЂ” what makes stack skills different from individual skills
    49в†’
    50в†’## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    51в†’
    52в†’**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    53в†’
    54в†’### 1. Generate Library Pairs
    55в†’
    56в†’From `confirmed_dependencies`, generate all unique pairs:
    57в†’- N libraries в†’ N*(N-1)/2 pairs
    58в†’- Skip pairs where either library had extraction failure in step 04
    59в†’
    60в†’Report: "**Analyzing {pair_count} library pairs for integration patterns...**"
    61в†’
    62в†’### 2. Detect Co-Import Files
    63в†’
    64в†’For each library pair (A, B):
    65в†’
    66в†’**Launch a subprocess** that greps across all source files to find files importing BOTH library A and library B. Return only file paths and import line numbers.
    67в†’
    68в†’**Subprocess returns:** `{pair: [A, B], co_import_files: [{path, line_A, line_B}], count: N}`
    69в†’
    70в†’**If subprocess unavailable:** Intersect the file lists from step 03 import counts for each pair.
    71в†’
    72в†’**Threshold:** A pair must have 2+ co-import files to qualify as an integration pattern (single file co-imports may be incidental).
    73в†’
    74в†’### 3. Classify Integration Types
    75в†’
    76в†’Load `{integrationPatterns}` for classification rules.
    77в†’
    78в†’For each qualifying pair (2+ co-import files), analyze the co-import files to classify the integration type:
    79в†’
    80в†’- **Type 1: Middleware Chain** вЂ” Sequential function calls piping output between libraries
    81в†’- **Type 2: Shared Types** вЂ” Type definitions exchanged between libraries
    82в†’- **Type 3: Configuration Bridge** вЂ” One library configuring or initializing another
    83в†’- **Type 4: Event Handler** вЂ” Event patterns crossing library boundaries
    84в†’- **Type 5: Adapter/Wrapper** вЂ” Thin wrapper connecting library interfaces
    85в†’- **Type 6: State Sharing** вЂ” Shared state stores or context providers
    86в†’
    87в†’For each detected integration:
    88в†’- Identify the top 3 files demonstrating the pattern
    89в†’- Extract a brief description of how the libraries connect
    90в†’- Assign confidence: T1 if AST-verified, T1-low if source reading
    91в†’
    92в†’### 4. Build Integration Graph
    93в†’
    94в†’Assemble the integration graph:
    95в†’- **Nodes:** Confirmed libraries (with extraction data from step 04)
    96в†’- **Edges:** Detected integration pairs with type, file count, and description
    97в†’- Identify **hub libraries** (connected to 3+ other libraries)
    98в†’- Identify **cross-cutting patterns** (patterns spanning 3+ libraries)
    99в†’
   100в†’### 5. Display Integration Summary
   101в†’
   102в†’**If integrations detected:**
   103в†’
   104в†’"**Integration detection complete.**
   105в†’
   106в†’**Integration graph:** {lib_count} libraries, {pair_count} integration pairs
   107в†’
   108в†’**Hub libraries** (connected to 3+ others):
   109в†’{For each hub:} - **{library}** вЂ” integrates with {partner_list}
   110в†’
   111в†’**Detected integrations:**
   112в†’| Library A | Library B | Type | Co-import Files | Confidence |
   113в†’|-----------|-----------|------|-----------------|------------|
   114в†’| {name} | {name} | {type} | {count} | {tier} |
   115в†’
   116в†’{If cross-cutting patterns:}
   117в†’**Cross-cutting patterns:**
   118в†’- {description spanning 3+ libraries}
   119в†’
   120в†’**Proceeding to stack compilation...**"
   121в†’
   122в†’**If no integrations detected:**
   123в†’
   124в†’"**No co-import integration patterns detected** between confirmed libraries.
   125в†’
   126в†’The libraries in this project appear to operate independently. The stack skill will contain library summaries without an integration layer.
   127в†’
   128в†’**Proceeding to stack compilation...**"
   129в†’
   130в†’### 6. Auto-Proceed to Next Step
   131в†’
   132в†’Load, read the full file and then execute `{nextStepFile}`.
   133в†’
   134в†’---
   135в†’
   136в†’## рџљЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   137в†’
   138в†’### вњ… SUCCESS:
   139в†’
   140в†’- All library pairs analyzed for co-imports
   141в†’- Integration types classified using pattern rules
   142в†’- Hub libraries and cross-cutting patterns identified
   143в†’- Integration graph built with confidence labels
   144в†’- Graceful handling of zero integrations (not a failure)
   145в†’- Auto-proceeded to step 06
   146в†’
   147в†’### вќЊ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   148в†’
   149в†’- Fabricating integrations not evidenced in code
   150в†’- Not applying the 2+ file threshold for pairs
   151в†’- Starting to compile SKILL.md (step 06's job)
   152в†’- Treating zero integrations as a workflow failure (it's valid)
   153в†’
   154в†’**Master Rule:** Only document integrations that exist in actual code. Zero integrations is a valid result.
   155в†’

1в†’# skills-ref
     2в†’
     3в†’Reference library for Agent Skills.
     4в†’
     5в†’> [!IMPORTANT]
     6в†’> This library is intended for demonstration purposes only. It is not meant to be used in production.
     7в†’
     8в†’## Installation
     9в†’
    10в†’### macOS / Linux
    11в†’
    12в†’Using pip:
    13в†’
    14в†’```bash
    15в†’python -m venv .venv
    16в†’source .venv/bin/activate
    17в†’pip install -e .
    18в†’```
    19в†’
    20в†’Or using [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/):
    21в†’
    22в†’```bash
    23в†’uv sync
    24в†’source .venv/bin/activate
    25в†’```
    26в†’
    27в†’### Windows
    28в†’
    29в†’Using pip (PowerShell):
    30в†’
    31в†’```powershell
    32в†’python -m venv .venv
    33в†’.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
    34в†’pip install -e .
    35в†’```
    36в†’
    37в†’Using pip (Command Prompt):
    38в†’
    39в†’```cmd
    40в†’python -m venv .venv
    41в†’.venv\Scripts\activate.bat
    42в†’pip install -e .
    43в†’```
    44в†’
    45в†’Or using [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/):
    46в†’
    47в†’```powershell
    48в†’uv sync
    49в†’.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
    50в†’```
    51в†’
    52в†’After installation, the `skills-ref` executable will be available on your `PATH` (within the activated virtual environment).
    53в†’
    54в†’## Usage
    55в†’
    56в†’### CLI
    57в†’
    58в†’```bash
    59в†’# Validate a skill
    60в†’skills-ref validate path/to/skill
    61в†’
    62в†’# Read skill properties (outputs JSON)
    63в†’skills-ref read-properties path/to/skill
    64в†’
    65в†’# Generate <available_skills> XML for agent prompts
    66в†’skills-ref to-prompt path/to/skill-a path/to/skill-b
    67в†’```
    68в†’
    69в†’### Python API
    70в†’
    71в†’```python
    72в†’from pathlib import Path
    73в†’from skills_ref import validate, read_properties, to_prompt
    74в†’
    75в†’# Validate a skill directory
    76в†’problems = validate(Path("my-skill"))
    77в†’if problems:
    78в†’    print("Validation errors:", problems)
    79в†’
    80в†’# Read skill properties
    81в†’props = read_properties(Path("my-skill"))
    82в†’print(f"Skill: {props.name} - {props.description}")
    83в†’
    84в†’# Generate prompt for available skills
    85в†’prompt = to_prompt([Path("skill-a"), Path("skill-b")])
    86в†’print(prompt)
    87в†’```
    88в†’
    89в†’## Agent Prompt Integration
    90в†’
    91в†’Use `to-prompt` to generate the suggested `<available_skills>` XML block for your agent's system prompt. This format is recommended for Anthropic's models, but Skill Clients may choose to format it differently based on the model being used.
    92в†’
    93в†’```xml
    94в†’<available_skills>
    95в†’<skill>
    96в†’<name>
    97в†’my-skill
    98в†’</name>
    99в†’<description>
   100в†’What this skill does and when to use it
   101в†’</description>
   102в†’<location>
   103в†’/path/to/my-skill/SKILL.md
   104в†’</location>
   105в†’</skill>
   106в†’</available_skills>
   107в†’```
   108в†’
   109в†’The `<location>` element tells the agent where to find the full skill instructions.
   110в†’
   111в†’## License
   112в†’
   113в†’Apache 2.0
   114в†’

1в†’# Development
     2в†’
     3в†’## Code Quality
     4в†’
     5в†’Format and lint with ruff:
     6в†’
     7в†’```bash
     8в†’uv run ruff format .
     9в†’uv run ruff check --fix .
    10в†’```
    11в†’
    12в†’## Testing
    13в†’
    14в†’Run tests with pytest:
    15в†’
    16в†’```bash
    17в†’uv run pytest
    18в†’```
    19в†’

1в†’"""Reference library for Agent Skills."""
     2в†’
     3в†’from .errors import ParseError, SkillError, ValidationError
     4в†’from .models import SkillProperties
     5в†’from .parser import find_skill_md, read_properties
     6в†’from .prompt import to_prompt
     7в†’from .validator import validate
     8в†’
     9в†’__all__ = [
    10в†’    "SkillError",
    11в†’    "ParseError",
    12в†’    "ValidationError",
    13в†’    "SkillProperties",
    14в†’    "find_skill_md",
    15в†’    "validate",
    16в†’    "read_properties",
    17в†’    "to_prompt",
    18в†’]
    19в†’
    20в†’__version__ = "0.1.0"
